title: The Thief’s Story – Flashcard Concept Mastery
author: Ruskin Bond

questions.question: Who is the narrator of 'The Thief’s Story'?
questions.explanation: The story is narrated in the first person by Hari Singh, a young thief.

questions.question: Why does Hari Singh change his name frequently?
questions.correct_answer: To avoid the police and former employers
questions.explanation: Changing names helps Hari Singh stay ahead of the law and continue stealing.

questions.question: Who is Anil?
questions.explanation: Anil is a simple man who earns irregularly by writing and trusts Hari Singh completely.

questions.question: Why does Hari Singh find it difficult to rob Anil?
questions.explanation: Anil’s honesty and trust make Hari Singh feel guilty about stealing from him.

questions.question: Where does Anil keep his money?
questions.explanation: Hari Singh notices Anil hiding the money under the mattress before stealing it.

questions.question: Why does Hari Singh return to Anil?
questions.correct_answer: He values education and Anil’s trust
questions.explanation: Hari Singh realises that learning to read and write is more valuable than stolen money.

questions.question: How does Anil react after the theft?
questions.explanation: Anil does not accuse or punish Hari Singh and continues to trust him.

questions.question: What does Anil give Hari Singh the next morning?
questions.explanation: The note, still wet from the rain, shows that Anil knows about the theft but forgives him.

questions.question: What role does education play in the story?
questions.explanation: Education becomes a turning point that encourages Hari Singh to change his life.

questions.question: What is the central message of the story?
questions.correct_answer: Trust and compassion can transform people
questions.explanation: The story shows that kindness and education can reform even a thief.

notes.title: Theme of Trust
notes.content: Anil’s complete trust acts as a moral force that changes Hari Singh’s behaviour.

notes.title: Importance of Education
notes.content: Learning to read and write gives Hari Singh hope for a respectable future.

facts.fact: The story is written in first-person narration.
The ending highlights forgiveness rather than punishment.
